title: Method of hyperspectral measurement
abstract: The present disclosure provides a method of providing a hyperspectral image of a scene comprising a point (p). The method comprises providing an imaging device (10, 10') comprising a two-dimensional image sensing unit (12, 13, 14) having a spectral characteristic, which varies along at least one direction in a plane parallel with an image sensor (13), acquiring a first two-dimensional image (11) of the scene, the first image comprising the point (p), wherein the spectral content of the scene varies along the direction as a consequence of the varying spectral characteristic of the image sensing unit (12, 13, 14), moving the imaging device (10, 10') and the scene relative to each other, acquiring a second two-dimensional image (I2) of the scene, the second image comprising the point (p), wherein the spectral content of the scene varies along the direction as a consequence of the varying spectral characteristic of the image sensing unit (12, 13, 14), identifying the point (p) in the second image (I2) as a second pixel at a position (x2, y2) depicting the point (p), providing a spectral vector (S(p)) of the point, and providing an updated spectral vector (S'(p)) of the point based on an applicability vector (A(p)) of the point (p), the spectral vector (S(p)) of the point, a spectral value (z2(p)) of the second pixel and an applicability vector (B(p)) of the second pixel.
claims:
Claims 1 . A method of providing a hyperspectral image of a scene comprising a point (p), comprising: providing an imaging device (10, 10') comprising a two-dimensional image sensing unit (12, 13, 14) having a spectral characteristic, which varies along at least one direction in a plane parallel with an image sensor (13), acquiring a first two-dimensional image (11 ) of the scene, the first image comprising the point (p), wherein the spectral content of the scene varies along the direction as a consequence of the varying spectral characteristic of the image sensing unit (12, 13, 14), moving the imaging device (10, 10') and the scene relative to each other, acquiring a second two-dimensional image (I2) of the scene, the second image comprising the point (p), wherein the spectral content of the scene varies along the direction as a consequence of the varying spectral characteristic of the image sensing unit (12, 13, 14), identifying the point (p) in the second image (I2) as a second pixel at a position (x2, y2) depicting the point (p), providing a spectral vector (S(p)) of the point, providing an updated spectral vector (S'(p)) of the point based on an applicability vector (A(p)) of the point (p), the spectral vector (S(p)) of the point, a spectral value (z2(p)) of the second pixel and an applicability vector (B(p)) of the second pixel. 2. The method as claimed in claim 1 , further comprising: providing the applicability vector A(p) of the point (p) to describe, with respect to predetermined wavelengths, the spectral characteristic of the image sensing unit at a position (xi, yi) where the point (p) is depicted. 3. The method as claimed in claim 1 or 2, further comprising: providing the applicability vector B(p) of the second pixel, said applicability vector B(p) describing, with respect to a predetermined 

 wavelength, the spectral characteristic of the image sensing unit at the second pixel. 4. The method as claimed in claim 3, further comprising providing an updated applicability vector (A'(p)) based on the applicability vector (A(p)) of the point (p) and the applicability vector (B(p)) of the second pixel. 5. The method as claimed in claim 4, wherein the updated applicability vector (A'(p)) is provided as a maximum, on an element by element basis, of the applicability vector (A(p)) of the point (p) and the applicability vector (B(p)) of the second pixel. 6. The method as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein identifying the point (p) comprises using the spectral vector (S(p)) of the point. 7. The method as claimed in claim 6, wherein the identifying comprises identifying the point based on a difference between the second spectral value (z2(p)) and the spectral vector (S(p)). 8. The method as claimed in claim 6 or 7, wherein the identifying comprises identifying the point based on the spectral vector (S(p)), the second spectral value (z2(p)), the applicability vector (A(p)) of the point and the applicability vector (B(p)) of the second pixel. 9. The method as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein identifying the point (p) in the second image (I2) comprises providing a model of a neighborhood of the point (p) in one of the images (11 , I2), and matching the model to the one of the images (11 , I2). 10. The method as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, further comprising determining a respective observation angle (a1 , a2) of the point (p) based on respective positions (x, y) of the point (p) in the first and 

 second images (11 , I2) and a reference distance, and determining a distance between the imaging device and the point (p) based on the angles and the reference distance. 1 1 . The method as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein providing a spectral vector (S(p)) of the point (p) comprises assigning a first spectral value (z1 (p)) of a first pixel depicting the point (p) in the first image. 12. The method as claimed in any one of claims 1 -10, wherein providing a spectral vector (S(p)) of the point comprises receiving the spectral vector (S(p)) already updated based on the first image (11 ). 13. The method as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the varying spectral characteristic is provided by a variable optical filter (12) arranged adjacent the image sensor (13) and wherein the method further comprises providing the applicability vector (Ai(p)) of the point (p) based on a transmittance of the optical filter at each wavelength (Ai) at the respective position (xi, y) of the image sensor (13) where the point (p) is identified. 14. The method as claimed in claim 13, wherein the filter varies continuously along the direction, preferably over at least 90 % of a sensor length in the direction. 15. The method as claimed in claim 13, wherein the filter presents at least two spaced-apart portions having the same transmittance. 16. The method as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the spectral vector (S(p)) of the point (p) is provided as an N- dimensional vector comprising a predetermined number of wavelengths (Î»Î¹ .. AN), wherein N preferably is 5-150. 17. The method as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein a respective spectral vector (S(p)) is provided for a predetermined number of points (p) in the scene, said predetermined number of points being sufficient to positively identify an object or absence of an object in the scene. 18. The method as claimed in any one of the preceding claims wherein acquiring the first and/or second image comprises acquiring an image wherein the point (p) occurs only once. 19. The method as claimed in any one of claims 1 -17, wherein acquiring the first and/or second image comprises acquiring an image wherein the point (p) occurs at least twice. 20. The method as claimed in claim 19, further comprising determining a respective observation angle (CM , 0.2) of the points (p) based on respective positions (x, y) of the occurrences of the point (p) in the first and/or second images (11 , I2) and a distance which is an intrinsic distance of the imaging device. 21 . The method as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the imaging device (10, 10') comprises an optical variable bandpass filter. 22. The method as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the method comprises interpolating at least one element of the spectral vector, which element corresponds to a wavelength for which measurement has been made with respect to the point. 23. The method as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, further comprising: if the point has moved more than one column on the image sensing unit (12, 13, 14) between the first and second images, 

 then estimating, preferably by interpolating, elements of the spectral vector S(p) corresponding to wavelengths where no measurement has been done. 24. The method as claimed in claim 22 or 23, wherein said estimation is performed based on the applicability vector. 25. A method of providing a hyperspectral 3D representation of an object, comprising: providing a plurality of spectrally varying images depicting the object, providing a 3D reconstruction of the object based on the plurality of spectrally varying images, and assigning a hyperspectral signature to a plurality of 3D points on a surface of the object. 26. The method as claimed in claim 25, wherein providing a 3D reconstruction of the object comprises, providing a set of intrinsic parameters for the camera, such as focal length, lens distortion, etc., providing a set of extrinsic parameters for the camera, such as camera position and orientation, and providing a set of 3D points on the surface of at least part of the object. 27. The method as claimed in claim 26, further comprising providing a 3D surface polygon model of at least part of the object. 28. The method as claimed in claim 26 or 27, further comprising, for at least some of the spectrally varying images, projecting the at least some of the 3D points onto an image plane of the spectrally varying image based on the extrinsic and intrinsic parameters, to determine respective image specific coordinates corresponding to said 3D points. 29. The method as claimed in any one of claims 25-28, further comprising: traversing at least some of the camera parameters for at least some of the reconstructed 3D points, identifying a matching 3D point were the projection of the 3D point on one of the spectrally varying images results in valid image coordinates, and associating an index of this spectrally varying image with coordinates of that 3D point. 30. The method as claimed in claim 29, further comprising reconstructing a hyperspectral signature of at least some of the 3D points by traversing the associations of spectrally varying image indexes for the 3D point to provide a spectral vector for the 3D point. 31 . The method as claimed in claim 30, further comprising interpolating elements of the spectral vector corresponding to wavelengths where no measurement can be found among the spectrally varying images. 32. The method as claimed in any one of claims 25-31 , wherein assigning a hyperspectral signature to one of the 3D points comprises: identifying the 3D point (p) in a first one of the spectrally varying images, identifying the 3D point (p) in a second one of the spectrally varying images as a second pixel at a position (x2, y2) depicting the 3D point (p) providing a spectral vector (S(p)) of the 3D point, providing an updated spectral vector (S'(p)) of the 3D point based on an applicability vector (A(p)) of the 3D point (p), the spectral vector (S(p)) of the 3D point, a spectral value (z2(p)) of the second pixel and an applicability vector (B(p)) of the second pixel. 33. An imaging device, comprising: a two-dimensional image sensor (13), presenting first and second mutually orthogonal directions, 

 a optical filter (12), arranged in an optical path to the image sensor (13), wherein the optical filter (12) is an optical variable bandpass filter, the transmittance of which varies along on the first direction and being constant along the second direction, wherein the optical filter extends over at least 90 %, preferably 95%, or preferably 99% of a length of the image sensor (13) in the first and second directions, respectively, and wherein the imaging device is arranged to acquire two-dimensional images (11 , I2) of a scene, wherein the spectral content of the scene as depicted on the images (11 , I2) varies along the first direction as a consequence of the varying transmittance of the optical filter (12). 34. The imaging device as claimed in claim 33, wherein the optical filter is continuous along the first direction. 35. The imaging device as claimed in claim 33, wherein the optical filter presents at least two spaced-apart portions having the same transmittance. 36. The imaging device as claimed in any one of claims 33-35, further comprising a lens, which is arranged in an optical path towards the image sensor, wherein said lens provides one focal point. 37. The imaging device as claimed in any one of claims 33-35, further comprising a lens or lens set, which is arranged in an optical path towards the image sensor, wherein said lens or lens set provides at least two focal points. 38. The imaging device as claimed in any one of claim 33-37, further comprising a processing device, which is configured to perform the method as claimed in any one of claims 1 -31 .
